zh.Watling Street, Wroxeter, Shropshire, En: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾萊希特
zh.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton是Philip Upton的女兒，於大約1513年出生在英國戴文宏尼頓。她於1533年與Sir John Forester of Watling Street在英國英格蘭史洛普爾萊希特結婚。他們至少是12個兒子和3個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Watling Street, Wroxeter, Shropshire, En: 英国英格兰史洛普爾萊希特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton是Philip Upton的女儿，于大约1513年出生在英国戴文宏尼頓。她于1533年与Sir John Forester of Watling Street在英国英格兰史洛普爾萊希特结婚。他们至少是12个儿子和3个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Upton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places in England called Upton, most of which are named with Old English upp ‘upper’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different origins, such as one in Essex, originally named with the phrase upp in tūne ‘up in the settlement’, i.e. the higher part of the settlement, and Upton Lovell (Wiltshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Ubba + Old English tūn. This name was brought to Ireland (Antrim) in 1598 by Henry Upton, from Upton in Devon.

variants.name: Uptain
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Upton  .

nameMeanings.name: Joyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently from the Norman male name Josce (Middle English Josse), which in turn is from Jodocus, a Latinized form of a Breton name, Iodoc, meaning ‘lord’, borne by a 7th-century Breton saint. The name was in use in England among Breton followers of William the Conqueror. However, although this was fairly common as a male given name in the Middle Ages, it had virtually died out by the 14th century. There is evidence of its use as a girl's name from the 16th century onwards in parishes with strong Puritan links, which suggests that it may have been associated with the vocabulary word joy; see Joy . It was strongly revived in the 19th century under the influence of popular fiction. It is borne by characters in Mrs Henry Wood's East Lynne ( 1861 ) and Edna Lyall's In the Golden Days ( 1885 ). Modern use may well have been influenced also by the common Irish surname derived from the medieval Norman male name. See also Joss .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton was born about 1513, in Honiton, Devon, England as the daughter of Philip Upton. She married Sir John Forester of Watling Street in 1533, in Wroxeter, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 12 sons and 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton est née environ 1513, à Honiton, Devon, Angleterre, fille de Philip Upton. Elle a épousé Sir John Forester of Watling Street en 1533, à Wroxeter, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 12 fils et 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton wurde um 1513 in Honiton, Devon, England, als Tochter von Philip Upton geboren. Sie hat 1533 in Wroxeter, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Forester of Watling Street geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 12 Söhne und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton è nata il circa 1513 nel Honiton, Devon, England come figlia di Philip Upton. Lei sposato Sir John Forester of Watling Street nel 1533, nel Wroxeter, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 12 figli e 3 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Joyce Uptonは約1513年にイギリスデボンホーニトンでPhilip Uptonの娘として生まれた。1533年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウロクセターでSir John Forester of Watling Streetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも12人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton(은)는 대략 1513년 영국 데폰 호니턴에서 Philip Upton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1533년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 록세터에서 Sir John Forester of Watling Street(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton nasceu aproximadamente 1513, em Honiton, Devon, Inglaterra filha de Philip Upton. Ela casou-se com Sir John Forester of Watling Street em 1533, em Wroxeter, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 12 filhos e 3 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton родилась около 1513 г. в местности хонитон, девон, Англия. Её отца звали Philip Upton. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Forester of Watling Street в 1533 г. в местности роксетир, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 3 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Joyce Upton nació aproximadamente 1513, en Honiton, Devon, Inglaterra como hija de Philip Upton. Se casó con Sir John Forester of Watling Street en 1533, en Wroxeter, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 12 hijos y 3 hijas.
